one_line_ask: Batch-retouch eight raw restaurant food photos to one cohesive premium color grade, straightened and cleaned up, then deliver desktop (16:9) and mobile (4:5) crops of each plus a review board for owner sign-off.
brief: Kantyna Nova, a modern Polish comfort-food restaurant and catering brand, is launching a new website and needs its food photography brought up to a premium, cohesive standard. The owner, Marta Zawadzka, has handed off eight raw phone-and-DSLR shots straight from a shoot day: two hero plates (a bowl of pierogi with browned onions, a golden schnitzel platter), a bright beet-and-goat-cheese salad, a hearty bowl of zurek soup, a slice of layered sernik cheesecake, a flat-lay of a catering grazing spread, a bar shot of a craft kompot drink, and a wide interior shot of the dining room. As-shot they are inconsistent and unappetizing: several are slightly tilted, most are flat and underexposed with a cool/greenish canteen-lighting cast, backgrounds are busy, and the colours drift from photo to photo so they would never read as one brand. The job is a pure retouch/grade edit (no layout, no new imagery): (1) straighten anything tilted and auto-tone every shot to a clean baseline; (2) establish ONE cohesive premium look on the lead hero — warm the white balance off the canteen green, lift the shadows, push vibrance so food reads rich and appetizing, boost contrast, and settle on a reusable fo
